chief_complaint: Sudden onset severe crushing chest pain radiating to left arm and jaw, associated with diaphoresis and nausea. Pain started 2 hours ago at rest.
history_of_present_illness: 62-year-old male with known hypertension and Type 2 diabetes presents with sudden onset severe chest pain described as crushing and pressure-like, 9/10 in severity, radiating to the left arm and jaw. Associated with profuse diaphoresis, nausea, and mild dyspnoea. No pleuritic component. No cough or haemoptysis. Pain began at rest 2 hours ago. Not relieved by antacids.
review_of_systems: Positive: chest pain, diaphoresis, nausea, dyspnoea. Negative: fever, cough, headache, syncope, palpitations prior to pain onset.
current_medications: Metformin 1g BD, Amlodipine 10mg OD, Atorvastatin 40mg ON, Aspirin 75mg OD
